title: Jobs Filters Current Desired Mapping Collection

governing.notesMd: Mappings are the governing comparison surface between current and desired UX.

desiredRefs.title: Clear Filters resets active browse filters

items.summary: Current Clear Filters behavior is being clarified into a desired broad reset behavior, but confirmation is required before implementation.

preserved: Clear remains the user's reset action.
changed: Desired behavior explicitly resets active browse filters and may require explicit confirmation copy.
added: Confirmation and test gates are made explicit.
behaviorDelta.notesMd: This mapping is the decision surface; implementation cannot treat the desired row as governing until confirmation is resolved.

recommendation.summary: Confirm Clear Filters as an explicit broad reset with clear copy.
recommendation.rationaleMd: Clear/reset actions are high-risk because users can lose context; explicit confirmation reduces surprise.

alternatives.tradeoffsMd: Lower behavior-change risk, but ambiguity remains.

alternatives.summary: Split Clear Draft and Clear Active Filters into separate actions.
alternatives.tradeoffsMd: More precise, but adds UX complexity and requires a larger design decision.

recommendation.consequencesMd: If confirmed, implementation and tests must prove broad reset and cancellation behavior.

confirmation.notesMd: User confirmation required because this may change existing reset semantics.

assertions.summary: Confirmed clear behavior resets active filters and visibly broadens results.
Canceling the clear confirmation preserves active filters.

confirmationBaseline.notesMd: Backfilled as the audit baseline for the one-pass UX spec creation/update. This records provenance only; it does not replace UX authority, product confirmation, behavior mappings, or runtime drift ledgers.

desiredRefs.title: Apply commits a valid filter draft

items.summary: Apply behavior is treated as preserved and clarified by desired UX requirements.

preserved: Apply commits valid filter edits to live browsing.
added: Visibility, disabled state, and must-not-save behavior are explicitly specified.
behaviorDelta.notesMd: The empty current side shows the same mapping shape can represent evidence stored elsewhere.

recommendation.summary: Use desired Apply behavior as governing implementation truth.
recommendation.rationaleMd: The behavior is confirmed and high-value for refactor preservation.
recommendation.consequencesMd: Implementation must satisfy Apply visibility, enabled state, effects, and proof assertions.

assertions.summary: Apply commits valid edits and closes the modal.

confirmationBaseline.notesMd: Backfilled as the audit baseline for the one-pass UX spec creation/update. This records provenance only; it does not replace UX authority, product confirmation, behavior mappings, or runtime drift ledgers.
